zh.lifeSummary: 當Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE於1046年出生在法國i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌艾福利可貝爾-頌-斯特時，她的父親Bouchard de Corbeil II22歲，母親Countess Adélaïde De Crécy19歲。她於1071年之後與Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery結婚。他們至少是4個女兒的父母。她去世於1104年10月12日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯古內艾-素-馬內享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE于1046年出生在法国i萊-德-福朗斯艾頌艾福利可貝爾-頌-斯特时，她的父亲Bouchard de Corbeil II22岁，母亲Countess Adélaïde De Crécy19岁。她于1071年之后与Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery结婚。他们至少是4个女儿的父母。她去世于1104年10月12日在法国i萊-德-福朗斯瑟-賽吉登尼斯古內艾-素-馬內享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: CORBEILLE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Corneille, from Latin Cornelius .

variants.name: Cornelius
meanings: Dutch, German, Danish, and southern English: from the Latin personal name Cornelius (originally an old Roman surname, probably derived from cornu ‘horn’), borne by a 3rd-century pope and Christian saint. In England, this name is rarely found before the 16th century, when the name was brought to England from the Low Countries by Protestants, among whom it was also a popular name in the vernacular form Cornelis. In North America, this surname may also be an altered form of the German or Dutch variant Kornelius.

nameMeanings.name: Adélaïde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin (via French Adélaïde ), from adal ‘noble’ + heid ‘kind, sort’. It was borne in the 10th century by the wife of the Holy Roman Emperor Otto the Great. She became regent after his death and was revered as a saint. The given name increased in popularity in England during the 19th century, when it was borne by the wife of King William IV; she was the daughter of the ruler of the German duchy of Saxe-Meiningen. The Australian city of Adelaide was named in her honour.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE was born in 1046, in Corbeil-Essonnes-Ouest, Évry, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, her father, Bouchard de Corbeil II, was 22 and her mother, Countess Adélaïde De Crécy, was 19. She married Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery after 1071. They were the parents of at least 4 daughters. She died on 12 October 1104, in Gournay-sur-Marne, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE est née en 1046, à Corbeil-Essonnes-Ouest, Évry, Essonne, Île-de-France, France, son père, Bouchard de Corbeil II, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Countess Adélaïde De Crécy, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery après 1071. Ils ont eu au moins 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 12 octobre 1104, à Gournay-sur-Marne, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE 1046 in Corbeil-Essonnes-Ouest, Évry, Essonne, Île-de-France, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Bouchard de Corbeil II 22 und ihre Mutter Countess Adélaïde De Crécy 19. Sie hat nach 1071 Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 12. Oktober 1104 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Gournay-sur-Marne, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE è nata il nel 1046 nel Corbeil-Essonnes-Ouest, Évry, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, sua padre, Bouchard de Corbeil II, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Countess Adélaïde De Crécy, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery dopo il 1071. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figlie. Morì il 12 ottobre 1104, nel Gournay-sur-Marne, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLEが1046年にフランスiレ-デ-フランスエソンevリーコルベイル-エソネス-オウエストで生まれた時，父Bouchard de Corbeil IIは22歳，母Countess Adélaïde De Crécyは19歳であった。1071年以後にGuy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlheryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1104年10月12日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスセアイネ-サイント-デニスゴウルナイ-スル-マルンで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE(이)가 1046년 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 에손 e브리 코르브이-손스-우스트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Bouchard de Corbeil II(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Countess Adélaïde De Crécy(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1071년 이후 Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1104년 10월 12일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 세이인-세인트-데니스 고우른아이-수-마니에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE nasceu em 1046, em Corbeil-Essonnes-Ouest, Évry, Essonne, Île-de-France, França, seu pai, Bouchard de Corbeil II, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Countess Adélaïde De Crécy, tinha 19 anos. Ela casou-se com Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery após 1071. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 12 de outubro de 1104, em Gournay-sur-Marne, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, França, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE родилась в 1046 г. в местности корбил-ессоннес-оуест, eври, эссонн, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция, её отцу по имени Bouchard de Corbeil II было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Countess Adélaïde De Crécy, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery после 1071 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 дочери. Она умерла 12 октября 1104 г. в местности гоурнеи-сур-марн, сине-сеинт-денис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adélaïde Elisabeth DE CORBEILLE nació en 1046, en Corbeil-Essonnes-Ouest, Évry, Essonne, Île-de-France, Francia, su papá, Bouchard de Corbeil II, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Countess Adélaïde De Crécy, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Guy II  aka Le Rouge de Montlhery después de 1071. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijas. Murió el 12 de octubre de 1104, en Gournay-sur-Marne, Seine-Saint-Denis, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 58 años.
